description: Architectural patterns and mental blueprints for building combat and damage systems in UE5

entries.title: Pattern: Health & Damage System
entries.description: How to structure a health and damage system using components so any Actor in your game can take and deal damage without deep inheritance chains.
entries.whatItIs: A reusable architecture for health, damage, and death. The core idea: health is a component, damage is delivered through UE5's damage framework, and death is an event that the owning Actor decides how to handle.
whenToUse: Any game where actors can take damage and die (players, enemies, destructibles, vehicles)
When multiple different Actor types need health (don't duplicate logic per class)
When you want designers to tweak health values per Actor in the editor
whenNotToUse: If you're using GAS — use AttributeSets for health instead, the damage pipeline is different
Extremely simple games where only one thing has health (just put it directly on the class)
keyPoints: STEP 1 — Create a UHealthComponent (inherits UActorComponent). It owns: MaxHealth, CurrentHealth, and an OnHealthChanged delegate.
STEP 2 — The HealthComponent listens for damage. Override the owning Actor's TakeDamage or bind to OnTakeAnyDamage inside the component's BeginPlay.
STEP 3 — When health reaches zero, broadcast an OnDeath delegate. The component does NOT decide what death looks like — it just announces it.
STEP 4 — The owning Actor (enemy, player, barrel) listens to OnDeath and handles its own death: play animation, ragdoll, drop loot, respawn, whatever.
STEP 5 — For dealing damage, use UGameplayStatics::ApplyDamage or ApplyPointDamage. This goes through UE5's built-in damage pipeline.
STEP 6 — Expose CurrentHealth and MaxHealth as BlueprintReadOnly so UI widgets can bind to them directly.
STEP 7 — For multiplayer: replicate CurrentHealth with ReplicatedUsing and let the server be authoritative on all damage calculations.
commonMistakes: Putting health logic directly in ACharacter and then copy-pasting it to enemies, destructibles, etc. — use a component
Having the HealthComponent destroy the Actor on death — the Actor should decide its own death behavior
Applying damage on the client instead of the server in multiplayer — damage must be server-authoritative
Coupling the HealthComponent to a specific Actor type (casting to AMyCharacter inside the component) — keep it generic
Forgetting to clamp health between 0 and MaxHealth — negative health or health above max causes bugs
Not using UE5's damage framework (TakeDamage/ApplyDamage) and instead calling custom functions directly — you lose DamageType support and engine integration
goodPractices: HealthComponent only manages numbers and broadcasts events — no visual logic, no game logic
Use delegates (OnHealthChanged, OnDeath) so other systems can react without tight coupling
Support damage types (UDamageType) so you can have fire damage, poison damage, etc. with different behaviors
Add an Invincible bool on the component for easy designer control (cutscenes, spawn protection)
Keep the component self-contained — it should work on any Actor without knowing what that Actor is
